trajectory.description: Observation: Empty workspace, need to build full pipeline. Analysis: iWildCam is a spatial OOD task with long-tailed classes; CLIP ViT-L/14 provides strong pretrained features. Decision: Build CLIP-based classifier with class-balanced sampling, train 3 epochs for baseline, submit eval to measure iteration time.

Observation: Code files exist but train.py is truncated, no training has run yet. Need complete pipeline. Analysis: Must write complete train.py, verify all files work, and train for 3 epochs using 4 GPUs with DDP. CLIP ViT-L/14 (304M params) is within budget. Class-balanced sampling is critical for macro-F1 on long-tailed data. Decision: Write complete code, launch training with torchrun on 4 GPUs, 3 epochs, then submit eval to measure baseline and iteration time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training completed 3 epochs in ~27 min. Val F1=0.3262, train_acc=23%, val_acc=18.5%. The low accuracy is expected with class weights (prioritizing rare classes for macro-F1). The very high loss values (78.93 train, 146.73 val) suggest class weights may be too extreme. Analysis: Need to submit eval for baseline test score, then improve training. Key issues: class weights too aggressive (linear inverse frequency), only 3 epochs. Decision: Submit eval for current checkpoint to measure baseline + iteration time, then train longer with improved class weights (sqrt inverse frequency) and more epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: baseline macro_f1=0.2944, train_acc=23%, very high loss (78.93). Class weights using linear inverse frequency are too extreme, causing instability. Analysis: Need sqrt inverse frequency weights, better augmentation (RandomResizedCrop), LR warmup, and longer training. Decision: Rewrite train.py with improvements, train 10 epochs (~90 min), submit eval.

Observation: Training crashed with SIGABRT (OOM) on rank 1. Train.py was truncated in workspace. Analysis: batch_size=32 per GPU with CLIP ViT-L/14 is too memory-heavy. Need smaller batch size, gradient accumulation, and a complete non-truncated script. Decision: Rewrite train.py with batch_size=16, grad_accum=2, simplified augmentation, and ensure complete file. Also reduce num_workers to save RAM.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training completed 10 epochs, val_f1 improved from 0.3262 (epoch 3) to 0.3778 (epoch 10), but train_acc=95.4% vs val_acc=61.9% shows heavy overfitting. Training took ~2.4h for 10 epochs. Previous test macro_f1 was 0.2944. Analysis: Need to (1) submit eval for current best checkpoint to get test score, (2) add TTA to predict.py for free boost, (3) add EMA and stronger augmentation to reduce overfitting, (4) reduce training time per epoch. Decision: Submit eval now, then improve predict.py with TTA and retrain with EMA + MixUp + stronger regularization.

trajectory.description: Observation: macro_f1=0.3789, heavy overfitting (95.4% train vs 61.9% val). predict.py currently uses TTA=1 by default. Analysis: TTA can provide a free boost without retraining. Then need EMA + class-balanced oversampling + stronger regularization for retraining. Decision: Update predict.py with TTA=5 default and submit eval with current checkpoint for immediate boost. Also prepare improved train.py with EMA for next iteration.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best macro_f1=0.3789 with fine-tuned CLIP ViT-L/14, severe overfitting (95.4% train vs 61.9% val). 1.67h remaining. Analysis: Zero-shot CLIP provides domain-invariant features by construction - ensembling fine-tuned predictions with zero-shot CLIP predictions could improve OOD generalization without retraining. Adding hflip TTA may also help. Decision: Update predict.py with zero-shot CLIP ensemble + hflip TTA, check class names to verify they're meaningful for zero-shot, submit eval with current checkpoint.

, in main
    train_features, train_labels = extract_features(clip_model, train_loader, device)
  File "/workspace/train_linear.py", line 36, in extract_features
    features = features / features.norm(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
AttributeError: 'BaseModelOutputWithPooling' object has no attribute 'norm'
trajectory.description: Observation: Best macro_f1=0.3789 with fine-tuned CLIP, but severe overfitting (95.4% train vs 61.9% val). Zero-shot ensemble slightly hurt. ~57 min remaining. Analysis: The overfitting is in the backbone adaptation - frozen CLIP features should generalize much better OOD. A linear probe on frozen CLIP features is fast to train (feature extraction ~5-10 min, linear head ~1 min) and should reduce the overfitting gap dramatically. Decision: Train linear probe on frozen CLIP features with class-balanced loss, then submit eval. If it beats 0.3789, great; if not, I still have time for another attempt.

Observation: Linear probe training crashed because clip_model.get_image_features() returns a BaseModelOutputWithPooling object, not a tensor. Need to use vision_model + visual_projection directly. ~47 min remaining. Analysis: Quick fix to feature extraction, then train linear probe which should take ~15 min for feature extraction + 1 min for linear head training. This frozen-feature approach should reduce overfitting vs fine-tuning. Decision: Fix train_linear.py with correct CLIP feature extraction API, run it, submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Linear probe training produced extremely poor results (val_f1=0.0046) - essentially broken, likely due to truncated class_weights code or wrong hyperparameters. The fine-tuned model remains best at 0.3789. ~20 min remaining. Analysis: The cached CLIP features (129809x768 train, 14961x768 val) are valid. A sklearn LogisticRegression on these features should work well and be fast. This avoids the PyTorch training bugs. Decision: Write a quick sklearn-based linear probe script using cached features, save compatible checkpoint, and submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Linear probe without class_weight achieved val_f1=0.3955 (better than fine-tuned model's val_f1=0.3778). Best test macro_f1 so far is 0.3789 (fine-tuned). Only ~7.9 minutes remaining. Analysis: The linear probe on frozen CLIP features may generalize better OOD since it doesn't suffer from backbone overfitting. Need to submit eval for linear_best.pt ASAP. However, predict.py's linear probe path uses clip_model.get_image_features() which previously caused errors - need to fix to use vision_model + visual_projection approach that worked in train_linear.py. Decision: Fix predict.py for safe linear probe inference, submit eval for linear_best.pt immediately.
